Title: Glycerol-3-phosphate mediates rhizobia-induced systemic signaling in soybean

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for Rj2/Rfg1-mediated exclusion of incompatible rhizobia and for incompatible rhizobia-induced foliar resistance
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: systemic immune signaling against pathogens and undesirable symbionts
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; nodulation phenotyping; pathogen resistance assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not a direct metabolic engineering target for nutrient metabolite accumulation, but important for enabling immunity-linked symbiont selectivity
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: incompatible U122/U257 inoculation; Psg avrB treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Compromising RAR1 may permit undesirable nodulation and reduce disease resistance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as core because directly manipulated in Results and necessary for metabolite-linked phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RAR1 knockdown allows nodulation by incompatible rhizobia. 2. RAR1 is required for rhizobia-triggered systemic foliar resistance. 3. It acts upstream of the G3P-associated systemic immunity output.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for Rj2/Rfg1-mediated exclusion of incompatible rhizobia and associated systemic foliar resistance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; nodulation phenotyping; pathogen resistance assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports symbiont selectivity and immunity but is not a direct metabolite biosynthetic gene
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: incompatible rhizobial inoculation; Psg avrB treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss may weaken disease resistance and alter rhizobial selectivity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its requirement is notable because TIR-NB-LRR proteins often rely on EDS1 instead
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NDR1 knockdown breaks Rj2/Rfg1 incompatibility and permits active nodules by incompatible strains. 2. NDR1 is also required for pathogen- and rhizobia-induced systemic resistance. 3. It links ETI-like recognition with downstream G3P-associated defense signaling.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: L76-1988 (Rj2 rfg1); also grafting in Rj2 backgrounds
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: glycerol-3-phosphate biosynthesis / SAR-associated signaling metabolite pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: direct biosynthetic step for target signaling metabolite
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf; petiole exudate; root exudate/vascular stream
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in both foliar and root tissues; induced in leaves by incompatible U122 inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in leaves and roots; leaf transcript increased after incompatible rhizobium inoculation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for G3P accumulation and for strain-specific exclusion of incompatible rhizobia; also required for incompatible rhizobia-induced and pathogen-induced systemic foliar resistance
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported in this context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; metabolite quantification; yeast gpd1 complementation; grafting; exogenous G3P rescue; 14C-G3P transport assay; nodulation phenotyping; pathogen resistance assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: root inoculation with incompatible Bradyrhizobium japonicum USDA122 or compatible Sinorhizobium fredii USDA257; foliar infection with Psg avrB
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target to engineer selective rhizobial compatibility and systemic disease resistance, but manipulation may alter beneficial symbiosis with strain-specific outcomes; tissue-specific enhancement in shoots may be most effective
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated G3P signaling may restrict nodulation by some rhizobia; possible effects on symbiotic specificity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Leaf G3P biosynthesis, not root genotype for G3Pdh, was essential for exclusion; G3P moved from shoot to root mainly during incompatible interaction and some transported label appeared as derivatives/glycerol
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Glyma02g38310 encodes a functional G3P dehydrogenase, confirmed by yeast complementation. 2. Its leaf expression is induced by incompatible rhizobium USDA122. 3. Silencing abolishes leaf G3P accumulation, permits nodulation by incompatible rhizobia, and blocks rhizobia-induced systemic pathogen resistance. 4. Grafting and exogenous application show shoot-produced G3P is required and can suppress incompatible rhizobial nodulation when transported to roots.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: L76-1988 (Rj2 rfg1); Harosoy (Rpg1-b) in SAR assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: converts chorismate to isochorismate in salicylic acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for pathogen-induced and incompatible rhizobia-induced systemic foliar resistance, but not required for Rj2-mediated exclusion of incompatible rhizobia
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; nodulation phenotyping; pathogen resistance assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Psg avrB challenge; root inoculation with U122
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering SAR competence, but unlikely alone to control rhizobial exclusion trait
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbing SA biosynthesis may compromise disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because directly tested in Results as a metabolic comparator to G3P branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS knockdown did not break Rj2 incompatibility to U122. 2. ICS knockdown impaired systemic pathogen resistance induced by pathogen or incompatible rhizobia. 3. SA branch is dispensable for rhizobial exclusion but required for systemic foliar immunity.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: L76-1988 (Rj2 rfg1); Harosoy (Rpg1-b) in SAR assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: deaminates phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate, contributing to SA biosynthesis branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for pathogen-induced and incompatible rhizobia-induced systemic foliar resistance, but not for Rj2-mediated exclusion of incompatible rhizobia
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; nodulation phenotyping; pathogen resistance assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Psg avrB challenge; root inoculation with U122
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for enhancing SA-dependent systemic immunity, but not sufficient for rhizobial exclusion
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changing PAL may broadly affect phenylpropanoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly assayed as part of parallel SA branch in comparison with G3P branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAL knockdown did not alter nodulation exclusion of incompatible U122. 2. PAL knockdown impaired systemic resistance triggered by pathogen or incompatible rhizobia. 3. SA branch contributes to systemic immunity but not root incompatibility decision.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: systemic signaling leading to G3P biosynthesis and exclusion of incompatible rhizobia
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: immune recognition-dependent activation of systemic signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: G3P accumulation pathway; downstream defense signaling components
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: promotes induction of GmG3Pdh-associated G3P accumulation during incompatible interaction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Decisive determinant of incompatibility with USDA122 and trigger of shoot G3P-mediated systemic signaling
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: recognition of incompatible rhizobial strain/effectors such as NopP contextually discussed
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root genotype determines incompatibility and foliar resistance induction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables strain-specific exclusion of Bradyrhizobium japonicum USDA122 and induces foliar pathogen resistance associated with G3P accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: likely functions with RAR1; NDR1; HSP90/SGT1-associated immune machinery contextually tested, but direct complex not shown
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: natural genotype comparison; VIGS of signaling components; grafting; nodulation assay; pathogen resistance assay; metabolite measurement
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to control symbiont specificity and couple nodulation selectivity with systemic disease resistance; requires consideration of elite rhizobial compatibility
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May exclude potentially beneficial rhizobial strains and reduce nodulation options
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Recognition is root-driven, but exclusion requires shoot G3P biosynthesis and return transport to root
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rj2 genotype determines incompatibility to U122 in roots. 2. Incompatible interaction triggers systemic foliar resistance and G3P accumulation. 3. Root recognition alone is insufficient; shoot G3P production is required for effective exclusion.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: systemic signaling associated with incompatible rhizobial exclusion
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Determines incompatibility to USDA257/USDA205 background context and is linked to systemic resistance in incompatible interactions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes exclusion of incompatible rhizobia and induction of foliar resistance in rj2 Rfg1 plants
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: requires RAR1 and NDR1 for incompatibility phenotype in tests with U257-compatible/incompatible contexts
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genotype comparison; VIGS of RAR1/NDR1; nodulation assay; pathogen resistance assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for breeding selective rhizobial compatibility profiles
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could block nodulation by certain inoculant strains
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Less mechanistically dissected than Rj2 in this paper but treated as parallel core incompatibility regulator
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rfg1 mediates strain-specific incompatibility to certain rhizobia such as USDA257. 2. Incompatible interactions in Rfg1 genotype also induce systemic foliar resistance. 3. Shared ETI-like signaling components are required for this incompatibility response.
